artifacts.claimText: For UVic administrative or operational work, University Systems states that Microsoft Copilot is the GenAI tool approved for use with UVic Data, while publicly available tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Perplexity are not approved for highly confidential, confidential, or internal UVic Data.

artifacts.claimText: UVic's GenAI position statement says there is no general ban on GenAI tools including ChatGPT in learning and teaching, supports appropriate course-level integration, and says instructors are encouraged to provide clear course-outline instructions, but instructors are not permitted to use GenAI tools to grade student work at this time.

Approved as a candidate with narrow wording around learning and teaching, course-level instructor discretion, and the stated grading restriction.

artifacts.title: University of Victoria (UVic) AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official UVic sources provide public AI guidance across administration, teaching and learning, research, approved Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat use, and DeepSeek restrictions. The staged claims remain candidate-level and are scoped to the source language; no claim is marked canonical or human reviewed.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The ATWP page was not used for university-wide claims because it applies to a program/class context.
UVic internal search query URLs were not crawled because robots.txt disallows search query patterns.
